headline: Will Finney seals the BFL crown as Phil Johnson storms the final gameweek
summary: Will Finney and n0n_l0c4l.exe finished the job in GW38, ending on 2361 points and 67 clear of Chris Beeden’s Toon Park Rangers. Phil Johnson grabbed the final Manager of the Week honours with a superb 86, but the title race belongs to Will after a closing 71 and the hottest three-game finish in the league.
report:
That’s it: GW38 is done, the season is over, and Will Finney is the Biddenham Fantasy League champion. n0n_l0c4l.exe closed on 2361 after a 71, with Chris Beeden’s Toon Park Rangers second on 2294, 67 back. No late twist, no lead change, no final-day wobble — just Will seeing it out in style.

The top five all held their positions, but there was still plenty of noise. Mark Horton’s What the Van Hecke? finished third on 2276 after a 43, just 1 point ahead of olly barnes and Grasshoppers, who landed a massive 74 but still ended fourth on 2275. Mike Flynn’s Only Franks completed the top five on 2251 after a 57.

Manager of the Week went to Phil Johnson, whose Groß Misconduct signed off with a brilliant 86, no hits, and a 3-place climb. That was the best score in the league this week, ahead of olly barnes on 74 and Will Finney on 71. The BFL average was 45.6, ahead of the global average of 41, though the global highest was out there somewhere on 117.

Phil’s big final-week push had serious differential energy: Dorgu delivered 18 points, owned by just 2 managers, Ekene Amadi and Phil Johnson. Mavropanos also chipped in 8 for Hayley Phelan and Mark Horton, Taty scored 8 for David Tonks, and Palmer produced 8 for amy warden. Leno added 6 for James Marshall and Mark Horton, while Lammens gave James Geary 6.

Captaincy was where the final week got spicy. Haaland was the most popular captain with 9 backers and returned 0, which is a brutal way to end a season. Chris Beeden was named for the howler with Haaland on 0, while Mike Flynn nailed the genius call: B.Fernandes for 14 points with a multiplier of 2.

The template had a very mixed farewell. Haaland was owned by 20 managers, 69.0%, and scored 0. Semenyo, owned by 18, returned 6, while B.Fernandes rewarded 15 owners with 14. But Guéhi and O'Reilly, both owned by 13, scored 0, Rice gave 11 owners 0, Gabriel managed 1 for 12 owners, and Thiago returned 2 for 12 owners.

Chips were still flying on the final day. Will Finney played 3xc and still delivered 71, Georg Coakley used a wildcard for 38, and Ali Smith played bboost for 27. The season even ends with chips left unused across the league: 5 triple captains, 7 bench boosts and 8 free hits still sitting there.

There were a couple of hits too: Paul Alexis took the biggest one, an 8-point cost, turning 50 gross into 42 net. David Tonks took a 4-point hit and came out with 57 gross, 53 net. Meanwhile, Mike Flynn had the week’s bench agony with 6 points left sitting there.

At the wrong end of the weekly table, Callum Bowness took the wooden spoon with Bruno's Brothers on 17. That also meant a 2-place fall, while Emmy Rupasinghe dropped 1 after 37 and Jamie Mitchell dropped 1 after 22. On the way up, Phil Johnson climbed 3 with that 86, Alan Eckett-Brown climbed 1 with 42, and Shannon H climbed 1 with 35.

The tight little scraps stayed tight right to the end. Mark Horton finished just 1 ahead of olly barnes in ranks 3 and 4, James Geary ended 4 ahead of Cathy Eckett-Brown in ranks 8 and 9, and Hayley Phelan edged Abi Speight by 1 in ranks 11 and 12. Final-week margins, final-week bragging rights.

And if there was any doubt about who timed the run-in best, the form table settles it: Will Finney was the hottest manager over the last 3 with 245. Dan Birkinshaw was the coldest over the same stretch with 112. David Bailey’s season record survived the finale too: 124 in GW33 remains the big one.
